title: alchemilla vulgaris subsp. demissa
intro: Alchemilla vulgaris subsp. demissa is a subspecies of the common lady's mantle plant, a perennial herb belonging to the rose family. This plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and astringent properties. The health benefits of Alchemilla vulgaris subsp. demissa include treating digestive issues, soothing wounds and burns, and acting as a natural diuretic to help with urinary problems. Herbal preparations of this plant often come in the form of teas, tinctures, and infusions, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties and promote overall well-being.
